programmes.show_description: Olive's Divorce: Classic sitcom about a bus company. Stan takes Olive to the divorce courts as Arthur has now been gone for six months.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Time Out: Detective drama series. A prostitute sparks a murder hunt when she tells Kersey that one of her clients confessed to killing another prostitute, but how believable is she?
Feeding the Rat: West Country detective drama series. When a local climber is killed in a fall, an informant claims that he was pushed and a re-enactment adds to suspicions.
Music to Die For: Lewis and Hathaway investigate a link between the murder of a professor of German history and the recent winner of a local underground boxing bout.
The Great and the Good: A rape case appears to be solved after a murder, but nothing is what it seems when Lewis and Hathaway find the upper echelons of Oxford society closing ranks.
Midwife Crisis: Comedy-drama series. Martin fails to hit it off with Portwenn's new midwife when she tends to Louisa, and Bert is smitten with a new cook at his restaurant.
Do Not Disturb: Comedy-drama series. Joan starts a small bed and breakfast business to solve her farm's money problems, but she soon finds that her first guest is far from perfect.
And No Birds Sing: Drama series about two gardening enthusiasts turned amateur sleuths. A series of mysterious accidents befalls associates of Laura Thyme's estranged husband.
Arabica and the Early Spider: Light-hearted drama series. Rosemary and Laura get involved in a mystery from years before when they are hired to create a garden for a pop star.
Detective drama series. When a young woman dies in a busy park in broad daylight, the uncovering of her identity leads DS Dodds to confront secrets from his own past.
A Murder Is Announced: A murder is announced in the personal columns of a local paper, but is it just a game? Miss Marple visits and sets about uncovering the village's dark secrets.
They Seek Him Here: When a film about the French Revolution is made in Midsomer Magna, people start to lose their heads in the guillotine and it's not a film trick...
Death and Dreams: The drugging and killing of a depressive gambler in the quiet village of Midsomer Worthy reunites DCI Tom Barnaby with a doctor who used to profile criminals.
Suspenseful crime drama. Alison's risky tactics identify the gang's target, but her deception and the gang's escalating violence threaten to unravel her relationship with Liam.
Suspenseful crime drama. Alison joins Liam on a luxury trip where she scrabbles for clues about his plans while torn between her growing feelings for him and the police's doubts.
The Perfect Clippie: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings.
Shopping from home.
A Night to Remember: Classic British sitcom. Burglars in the neighbourhood lead to an eventful night for Oliver, Simon, Mrs Sadler and Banks when they are held at gunpoint.
On the Omnibuses: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. The company celebrates its centenary.
Viv faces a tense standoff with the police as Freddie grabs the twins' pram, she attempts to get help from Ross, but he finds the planted cash.
Liam faces up to his night with Carla, while Maria tells him she wants to register the baby's death and Gail has a run-in with Tina's mum when she turns up at the Platts'.
Rosie catches Liam sharing a kiss with Carla and films them on her mobile phone. Maria has a change of heart about her separation and Julie drives Fiz to breaking point.
The Quality of Mercy: Detective drama. When Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a rising Oxford theatre star, they soon focus their suspicions on his acting colleagues.
An American in Aidensfield: Police drama series set in 1960s Yorkshire. A stranger arrives in the village and goes into partnership with Greengrass.
Bringing It All Back Home: Drama series set in 1960s Yorkshire. Changes are in the air for Nick with the arrival of a new inspector, and Kate receives a tempting job offer.
Down Among the Dead Men: When a council clerk is shot dead, Barnaby and Jones discover that he was a blackmailer and investigate which of his victims hated him enough to kill him.
Jo wakes up bruised and Andy blames it on her drunken state the night before. Viv tells Bob she kissed Freddie, and Terry regrets letting the factory move its operations.
Nicola claims full credit for Rodney's book when she receives a call from a publisher. Bob opens up to Diane, and Terry decides he can't cope with the factory workers any more.
A weary Liam begins to yield under Maria's persuasive charms and reconsiders their relationship. David is surprised when Gail visits with Ted in tow and Carla buys Rosie's silence.
Sally plans the decor for her new home while Claire has second thoughts about the deal. Harry is visited by his ex-wife Clarissa and Michelle returns from Ireland.
Wild Thing: Nostalgic police drama. When some badly mauled sheep carcasses are discovered, Nick and Kate become involved in a hunt.
Witch Hunt: Police drama series set in the 1960s. Nick investigates a report by three children who claim to have seen a witch vandalising a funeral urn.
Death in a Chocolate Box: When Barnaby's former colleague is killed, he investigates a link between a halfway house for former prisoners and a seedy group called the Friday Nighters.
Detective drama series. McDonald and Dodds are sucked into the fast and furious world of Formula One after a promising young driver dies in suspicious circumstances.
The Quality of Mercy: Detective drama. When Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a rising Oxford theatre star, they soon focus their suspicions on his acting colleagues.
